news-global-econ.title: Grammy-Winning Singer Julio Iglesias Sues Spain's Deputy PM Diaz over Sexual Abuse Allegations
news-global-econ.description: Legendary Spanish singer Julio Iglesias sued Spain's Deputy Prime Minister Yolanda Díaz on Tuesday, accusing her of violating his "right to honor" over remarks she made about sexual abuse allegations against Iglesias brought by two former employees earlier th…

news-global-econ.title: The battle for Congress gets a Wall Street-style scoreboard powered by real-money bets
news-global-econ.description: Kalshi's new Midterms Hub combines live prediction markets with polling, fundraising data and historical results to track House, Senate and governor races.

news-global-econ.title: China is building a massive ship that should scare Americans about the future
news-global-econ.description: A massive replenishment ship near Guangzhou reveals China's strategy to sustain carrier groups far from home amid ongoing Taiwan tensions.

news-global-econ.title: STEVE FORBES, STEPHEN MOORE: America must free its airwaves before China pulls ahead
news-global-econ.description: The FCC's new "super band" auction could add $264 billion to gross domestic product and support 1.5 million jobs without imposing any new taxes.

news-global-econ.title: Rubio says US remains open to Iran talks but warns there 'will be consequences'
news-global-econ.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran faces consequences for violating commitments, saying Tehran demands illegal control over the Strait of Hormuz.

news-global-soc.title: SENATOR TIM SHEEHY: Crazy conspiracy theories are undermining America’s defense
news-global-soc.description: National Defense Authorization Act improves United States-Israel defense technology cooperation, which will help make American troops safer.

news-global-soc.title: Trump plays down prospects of Iran talks amid Red Sea threat
news-global-soc.description: Trump downplays Iran talks amid escalating tensions and threats to Red Sea shipping, as military actions and oil prices rise.

news-global-soc.title: India weighs letting airport owners run airlines to curb duopoly
news-global-soc.description: India considers allowing airport owners to operate airlines, potentially disrupting the duopoly held by IndiGo and Air India.
